nodes.label: AES-256
nodes.insight: AES-256-CBC provides authenticated encryption for inter-node communication. The IV prevents pattern analysis across encrypted flows.

nodes.label: IoT Security
nodes.insight: IoT devices generate heterogeneous telemetry that requires specialized feature engineering — standard network flow features don't transfer directly.

nodes.label: Distributed IDS
nodes.insight: 3.5M network flows across 10 attack classes. The ensemble approach (RF + XGBoost + Isolation Forest) achieved 99.7% — no single model gets above 97%.

nodes.label: Security Module
nodes.insight: The SECURITY module in AIN routes all vulnerability scanning and threat analysis through a dedicated Llama 3.1 405B instance via NVIDIA NIM.

nodes.label: XGBoost
nodes.insight: Best balance of accuracy (88%) and inference speed for IoT malware. Native handling of class imbalance via scale_pos_weight. Feature importance is interpretable for IEEE papers.

nodes.label: Random Forest
nodes.insight: 99.7% on network flows — the baseline that proved ensemble methods work for IDS. Parallelizable, interpretable, fast inference.

nodes.label: SMOTE
nodes.insight: Synthetic minority oversampling was critical — IoT malware classes ranged from 200 to 50K samples. Without SMOTE, the model biased toward majority classes.

nodes.label: Isolation Forest
nodes.insight: Unsupervised anomaly detection as a complementary signal. 94% on anomalies but can't classify — works best as a pre-filter in the ensemble.

nodes.label: ML Pipeline
nodes.insight: End-to-end pipeline: feature extraction → selection → model training → evaluation → deployment. Modular design allows swapping models without breaking the flow.

nodes.label: Flask API
nodes.insight: Lightweight REST API for model inference. Handles 500+ requests/sec on a single instance. The bottleneck is feature extraction, not the API layer.

nodes.label: ChromaDB
nodes.insight: Vector store for the MEMORY module. Embeds research notes, project logs, and past decisions. Enables semantic retrieval across the entire knowledge base.

nodes.label: Ollama
nodes.insight: Local inference for privacy-sensitive tasks. Runs Hermes 3 for the STRATEGY and ADVISOR modules. Zero cloud latency for reasoning tasks.

nodes.label: CUDA Computing
nodes.insight: Custom CUDA kernels for transformer attention. Mixed-precision (FP16/BF16) compute pipeline targeting 3x inference speedup over PyTorch default.

nodes.label: Multi-Agent AI
nodes.insight: 6 specialized modules: COMMAND, SECURITY, RESEARCH, STRATEGY, MEMORY, ADVISOR. Each module does one thing well. The orchestrator routes, not reasons.

nodes.label: UPI Research
nodes.insight: 70% of Gen Z UPI transactions under ₹500. Peak fraud window 7-10 PM. Neurofinance framework applied — cognitive biases map directly to transaction patterns.

nodes.label: HeatSense
nodes.insight: Fusing satellite thermal imagery with smartphone sensors and NDVI maps. The challenge is temporal alignment — satellite passes are periodic, phone data is continuous.

nodes.label: Feature Selection
nodes.insight: Hybrid feature selection achieved 57% dimensionality reduction (20 → 9 features) while maintaining 88% accuracy. The key insight: flow-level metadata alone classifies most IoT malware.

nodes.label: Adversarial ML
nodes.insight: Planned: Adversarial robustness testing for IDS models. How do evasion attacks affect detection accuracy?

nodes.label: Transformer Anomaly
nodes.insight: Planned: Transformer-based anomaly detection using self-attention over network flow sequences. Requires GPU infrastructure.

nodes.label: GPU Cluster
nodes.insight: Planned: Multi-node GPU orchestration for large-scale model training and federated learning across IoT devices.

researchNotes.title: CICFlowMeter Feature Engineering
researchNotes.content: 79 features per flow. Timing features (IAT) are most discriminative for DoS. Volume features distinguish data exfiltration.

researchNotes.title: SMOTE vs ADASYN for IoT
researchNotes.content: SMOTE performed better for IoT malware — ADASYN generates noisy samples near class boundaries. k=5 neighbors optimal.

researchNotes.title: HeatSense Satellite Pipeline
researchNotes.content: Landsat 8/9 thermal bands at 100m resolution. Temporal alignment with ground sensors requires interpolation.

researchNotes.title: XGBoost Hyperparameter Sweep
researchNotes.content: max_depth=6, n_estimators=200, learning_rate=0.1 optimal for IoT. Higher depth overfits on minority classes.

projectLogs.title: 3-Node IDS Deployment
projectLogs.content: Mumbai → Nagpur → Cloud. AES-256 encrypted channels. Flask API handles 500 req/sec.

projectLogs.title: 57% Feature Reduction
projectLogs.content: Hybrid selection: filter (mutual information) + wrapper (recursive feature elimination). 20 → 9 features.

projectLogs.title: 6-Module Architecture
projectLogs.content: COMMAND → SECURITY → RESEARCH → STRATEGY → MEMORY → ADVISOR. Each module独立, orchestrator routes.

projectLogs.title: CUDA Kernel Benchmarks
projectLogs.content: Custom attention kernel: 2.8x over PyTorch default. Mixed-precision pipeline in architecture phase.

lessonsLearned.detail: 99% accuracy on 99:1 imbalance means the model just predicts majority class. Use F1, precision, recall.
XGBoost with 9 features outperforms deep nets with 79. Simpler models generalize better on small security datasets.
Node failures in IDS caused cascading re-balancing. Consistent hashing fixed the hot-spot problem.
IEEE reviewers want to know WHY a model works. XGBoost feature importance > black-box neural nets for published research.

futureIdeas.title: Federated Learning for IoT Privacy
futureIdeas.content: Train models across IoT devices without centralizing data. Differential privacy guarantees.

futureIdeas.title: Transformer-Based Anomaly Detection
futureIdeas.content: Self-attention over flow sequences. Context window captures temporal attack patterns that tree methods miss.

futureIdeas.title: GPU Cluster Orchestrator
futureIdeas.content: Multi-node GPU task scheduling. Kubernetes for ML workloads. Auto-scaling based on training queue depth.
